pages.full_text:
Lord + Lady's solar chamber, battlements. Cellar with barrel marks on walls. Green, cosy. You can imagine a family living there - it would be a challenge to make it comfortable for a family today, after 250 years without residents.
	Arrived at __Hamperley__ campsite ****
Lovely big flat field, wood along one side. Extraordinary loo, put together free - corrugated, brown paper over floor, Space also used to store old cooker + T.V. Very useful because only 1¼ hours from B'ham. Very windy during the night.
	In the morning we visited Ludlow Castle. The boys waved from the battlements.. We walked around [?Ludlow].
	The year finished with a [illegible] at [?Tomislen].
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread. Text at top of page appears to be describing a castle visit (Lord & Lady's solar chamber, battlements, cellar) likely referring to Ludlow Castle or another castle visited. The campsite 'Hamperley' is underlined. The final line is partially cut off at the bottom; the last word could be 'Tomislen' or similar — hard to read.

pages.full_text:
1990

April 6th. Day out to Windmill + Chur[ch] with Doris + Adelbert,
	Simon, John, Charles + I came home a quick route, because of [chi]lds, leaving Doris + Adelbert + Thomas + Peter to Adelbert's map-
pages.notes: The page has a large pencil drawing of a windmill occupying most of the upper half, with '1990' written above it — this appears to be a section heading/illustration for the 1990 entries. Text at the bottom is partially cut off at the right edge and bottom of the image. 'Chur[ch]' — the word continues off the right edge, likely 'Church'. The word before 'lds' is partially cut off — likely 'chi' making 'childs' or possibly 'child's'. Text continues on the next page. No specific place names legible beyond the implied Windmill and Church destination.
